rules.statement: Components SHOULD follow UNIX philosophy: do one thing well, compose via clear interfaces, minimize coupling.
rules.rationale: Per ADR-042 D1, this rule is the responsibility-count verdict over candidates raised by modularity.class_too_large (the LOC pre-selector). LOC is not a finding in itself; line count merely identifies classes for review, and this rule answers whether the class genuinely carries multiple responsibilities (seam-large) versus presenting a single gateway responsibility (facade-large) or cohesive algorithm (algorithm-large). The dependency is wired in .intent/enforcement/mappings/architecture/modularity.yaml via `requires_findings_from: [modularity.class_too_large]`. Currently routed through LLMGateStubEngine pending audit-pipeline LLM client wiring (ADR-042 D4 / GitHub #306); the governed_exclusions register in .intent/enforcement/mappings/code/modularity.yaml provides the interim mechanism for facade-large and algorithm-large classes until this rule becomes operational.
